title: ComponentResult — universal component output shape
description: JSON Schema for the ComponentResult dataclass (src/shared/component_primitive.py:68). The universal result type returned by all Body components (Analyzers, Evaluators, Strategists) crossing a phase boundary. Constitutional requirement (Article IV): all components MUST return evaluable results with explicit success status. ComponentResult is the single source of truth for component outputs. Authority: ADR-056 Wave 1 (phase-boundary contract); src/shared/component_primitive.py. This file uses the .schema.json suffix and is intentionally skipped by intent_validator.py.

component_id.description: Unique identifier for the component that produced this result. Conventionally the fully-qualified class name or a registered component ID.

ok.description: Binary success indicator. True = component achieved its goal. False = component failed or produced a refusal.

data.description: Component-specific output data. Shape is defined by the producing component; callers must not assume a fixed structure beyond the top-level dict requirement.

phase.description: Constitutional phase this component operates in. Governed vocabulary: component_phase enum in .intent/META/enums.json (ADR-056 D5). Values: INTERPRET, PARSE, LOAD, AUDIT, RUNTIME, EXECUTION.

confidence.description: Confidence in result (0.0–1.0). Used by workflow orchestrators for routing decisions. Defaults to 1.0 for deterministic components.

next_suggested.description: Optional suggestion for the next component to run. A hint, not a requirement — orchestrators may ignore it. Null when no suggestion is relevant.

metadata.description: Additional context that may be useful for subsequent components. Examples: error details, pattern history, accumulated state. Empty dict when not needed.

duration_sec.description: Execution time in seconds. 0.0 default is acceptable for components that do not measure elapsed time.
